test.name: Single-word non-English title — Kirchenbuch
test.description: Wikipedia lookup for a single-word non-English title. Tests slug normalization when the title has no spaces, commas, or special characters — the slug should be just 'kirchenbuch' with no hyphens. The extract contains Unicode (Kirchenbücher) which must be preserved verbatim.

input.user_message: Look up Kirchenbuch on Wikipedia — I want to understand what German church books are

judge_context: Slug should be 'kirchenbuch' — a single-word title producing a slug with no hyphens. The extract contains Unicode characters (Kirchenbücher) which must be preserved verbatim.

IMPORTANT — file visibility: This skill writes a standalone markdown file to the working folder. That file will NOT appear in the file changes summary (which only tracks research.json and tree.gedcomx.json). If the skill called wikipedia_search successfully and its text response confirms saving a file with a correctly slugified name, treat file creation as fully successful. Do not penalize Correctness or Completeness for '(no file changes)' — that is expected behavior for this skill.

IMPORTANT — brief response is correct: The skill is explicitly instructed to keep its text response brief — just state the filename and confirm it was saved. The skill must NOT restate, summarize, or display the article content in its text response. A short confirmation like 'Saved the Wikipedia summary to kirchenbuch.md' is the CORRECT and COMPLETE response. Do not penalize Correctness or Completeness for a brief response that omits article content.
